sourceRef: tip:Making room for deep work between certifications

slides.eyebrow: Making room for deep work between certifications
slides.headline: Protect a focus block
slides.body: Create a dedicated time slot for focused work between VA School Certifying Officer (VASCO) tasks.

slides.headline: Define a clear purpose
slides.body: Set a specific goal for your deep work block, such as completing a COE review or processing 22-1999 forms.

slides.headline: Communicate the block to team
slides.body: Inform your colleagues about the dedicated focus block. Use phrases like 'During my deep work time, I’ll be unavailable for interruptions.'

slides.headline: Schedule the block
slides.body: Block out specific hours in your calendar. This could be, for example, 10:00 AM to 11:00 AM.

slides.headline: Eliminate distractions
slides.body: During your focus block, close unnecessary tabs and notifications. Consider setting up a 'do not disturb' mode.

slides.headline: Review and adjust
slides.body: At the end of each focus block, reflect on what you accomplished and adjust the schedule as needed.
Try scheduling a deep work block today. Link in bio.
slides.headline: Read the full post

outline.caption: Protect a dedicated focus block to enhance productivity and efficiency between VA School Certifying Officer (VASCO) tasks. Follow these steps: Define a clear purpose, communicate the block to team, schedule the time, eliminate distractions, and review and adjust. Try scheduling a deep work block today.

rubricBreakdown.critique: The weakest slide is Step 4, 'Eliminate distractions,' as it could benefit from a more detailed example or tip to help users better understand how to minimize distractions during their focus block.
